prompt: DevBoss 5-hour sprint continuation: exercise end-to-end-loop and caveman-code on locally available/provided repositories, especially tdrrecherche; stay read-only/dry-run for target repos; record evidence, blockers, learning candidates, and Copilot availability; validate changed end-to-end-loop artifacts; no live deploy or destructive target changes.
commands_or_evidence: Target feature worktree /opt/data/worktrees/tdrrecherche/feat-complete-wordpress-design-samples is present on branch feat/complete-wordpress-design-samples at bde99b7 with clean status; main worktree remains main at f45b2c9.
No .end-to-end-loop memory files were present in the target repo/worktree; PROJECT_STATE.md and README.md were read as handoff context.
Scenario chosen: read-only WordPress scaffold security/readiness audit, focused on required theme files, WordPress hooks, unsafe PHP patterns, prototype forms, and deployment blockers.
Tooling discovery: node v22.22.3 is available; php is not installed; GitHub CLI is authenticated but gh copilot prompt execution returned `! Copilot CLI not installed`.

WordPress scaffold audit found 10 PHP files, 1 JS file, 2 CSS files, theme headers present, after_setup_theme/wp_enqueue_scripts/wp_head/wp_footer/wp_body_open present, skip link present, nav fallback disabled.
Release-blocking readiness gap for actual WordPress packaging: required theme file index.php is missing from wordpress-theme/tdr-recherche/.

Security pattern checks found 0 raw superglobals, 0 eval/exec/shell_exec/system/passthru/popen/proc_open calls, 0 direct $wpdb query calls, 0 admin_post/wp_ajax handlers, 0 wp_mail calls, and 0 wp_remote calls in the theme scaffold.

Prototype form audit: sampler has 5 POST forms and each standalone variant has 1 POST form; all use action=# prototype targets, so production form/nonce/spam/SMTP integration remains a blocker rather than verified behavior.
Broad secret-pattern scan only matched safe PROJECT_STATE.md documentation references to token/secret scan history; theme-focused unsafe PHP pattern scans returned 0 hits.
node --check wordpress-theme/tdr-recherche/assets/js/theme.js passed; php -l remained blocked because php is not installed; git diff --check passed; target git status remained clean.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Discover target repo state and any .end-to-end-loop memory
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Feature worktree bde99b7 on feat/complete-wordpress-design-samples is clean; target .end-to-end-loop memory files were not present.
Selected WordPress scaffold security/readiness audit because the prior dry-run identified PHP/WordPress production verification as the key remaining risk.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Exercise the loop as far as safe with real commands/read-only checks
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Ran git discovery, WP theme file/hook audit, unsafe PHP pattern checks, form prototype audit, JS syntax check, diff check, and target status check without target writes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Record evidence, blockers, learning candidates, and Copilot availability
acceptance_criteria.evidence: This result log records the missing index.php blocker, php-lint blocker, production form-integration blocker, and Copilot CLI unavailability despite gh authentication.
This JSON result log is schema-compatible with the skill validator; follow-up validation commands are recorded in the final report.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Do not live deploy or destructively modify target repos
acceptance_criteria.evidence: No deploy, commit, destructive operation, package install, or target repo write was performed; target git status remained clean.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: WordPress scaffold is release-ready for production packaging
acceptance_criteria.evidence: index.php is missing and PHP syntax/WordPress runtime checks are unavailable locally; production contact-form integration is still unresolved.

learning_candidates.text: For WordPress scaffold readiness, include a required theme-file gate for style.css, functions.php, index.php, header.php, footer.php, and at least one render template before claiming package readiness.
learning_candidates.reason: Portable WordPress dry-run gate discovered a real packaging blocker; privacy-safe and broadly reusable.

learning_candidates.text: A scaffold with prototype contact forms using action=# is not production-ready until WordPress form handling, nonce/spam controls, SMTP/delivery, and privacy copy are chosen and verified.
learning_candidates.reason: Recurring WordPress/contact-form readiness blocker; prevents false live-deploy readiness claims.

learning_candidates.text: Copilot findings remain unavailable when `gh copilot -p ...` returns `! Copilot CLI not installed`, even if `gh auth status` is authenticated.
learning_candidates.reason: Environment-specific and already similar to prior Copilot availability notes.

learning_candidates.text: If Tijmen approves a narrow target repo fix later, add a minimal index.php fallback to the WordPress scaffold and verify with PHP lint/WordPress environment or CI.
learning_candidates.reason: Target-repo-specific next action; keep in result log rather than global memory.

copilot_findings.summary: GitHub CLI is authenticated, but the Copilot prompt path was unavailable: `gh copilot -p ...` exited with `! Copilot CLI not installed`.

notes: Read-only/dry-run audit found one concrete WordPress packaging blocker (missing index.php) and preserved target repo cleanliness. Only end-to-end-loop evaluation artifacts were written.
